responseMessageContents.text:
The user wants me to send them the Rork variables (environment variables) for the security box so they can share their GitHub and Render credentials to complete deployment. Let me look at the security box I built and understand what credentials/env vars are needed.

Let me check the security box and deployment tools to understand what environment variables need to be set.
I'll check the security box code to give you the exact list of Rork environment variables you need to set so the deployment can complete.

The user wants to set up the Rork environment variables for GitHub and Render credentials to complete deployment. Let me check the existing Rork env variables and request the specific credentials needed.

Looking at the credential-sync.ts, the required credentials are:
- GITHUB_TOKEN
- GITHUB_REPO_URL
- RENDER_API_KEY
- RENDER_SERVICE_ID

These need to be set as environment variables. Let me request them from the user via requestEnvs. These are private (server-side) secrets, so they should not be public EXPO_PUBLIC_ vars.

Let me request the exact credentials the Security Box needs to complete the deployment.

I'll request the exact Rork environment variables needed to wire GitHub + Render into the Security Box so deployment can complete. These are private server-side secrets — they will not be inlined into client code.

envs.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
envs.description: GitHub Personal Access Token (classic) with repo, workflow, and read:org scopes. Used to push commits, read repo status, and trigger workflows from the IVX backend.

envs.name: GITHUB_REPO_URL
envs.description: Full GitHub repository URL for the IVX platform (the repo that Render deploys from). Example: https://github.com/ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest

envs.name: RENDER_API_KEY
envs.description: Render API key with read+write scope. Used to read service status, list deploys, trigger new deploys, and sync env vars. Get it from Render → Account → API Keys.

envs.name: RENDER_SERVICE_ID
envs.description: Render service ID (srv-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx) for the IVX backend service. Found in Render → your service → Settings → Service ID.

envs.name: SUPABASE_URL
envs.description: Supabase project URL (https://xxxx.supabase.co). Used by the backend to verify auth and DB access. Required for full Security Box validation.

envs.name: SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
envs.description: Supabase service role key (secret). Used for admin DB operations and proof-ledger writes. Found in Supabase → Project Settings → API → service_role secret.

input.reason: These are the exact server-side secrets the IVX Security Box needs to verify GitHub + Render live, push commits, trigger deploys, and record proof. Without them the Security Box returns STATE: BLOCKED — missing GITHUB_TOKEN / RENDER_API_KEY / RENDER_SERVICE_ID.

requestedEnvs.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
requestedEnvs.description: GitHub Personal Access Token (classic) with repo, workflow, and read:org scopes. Used to push commits, read repo status, and trigger workflows from the IVX backend.

requestedEnvs.name: GITHUB_REPO_URL
requestedEnvs.description: Full GitHub repository URL for the IVX platform (the repo that Render deploys from). Example: https://github.com/ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest

requestedEnvs.name: RENDER_API_KEY
requestedEnvs.description: Render API key with read+write scope. Used to read service status, list deploys, trigger new deploys, and sync env vars. Get it from Render → Account → API Keys.

requestedEnvs.name: RENDER_SERVICE_ID
requestedEnvs.description: Render service ID (srv-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx) for the IVX backend service. Found in Render → your service → Settings → Service ID.

requestedEnvs.name: SUPABASE_URL
requestedEnvs.description: Supabase project URL (https://xxxx.supabase.co). Used by the backend to verify auth and DB access. Required for full Security Box validation.

requestedEnvs.name: SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
requestedEnvs.description: Supabase service role key (secret). Used for admin DB operations and proof-ledger writes. Found in Supabase → Project Settings → API → service_role secret.

output.reason: These are the exact server-side secrets the IVX Security Box needs to verify GitHub + Render live, push commits, trigger deploys, and record proof. Without them the Security Box returns STATE: BLOCKED — missing GITHUB_TOKEN / RENDER_API_KEY / RENDER_SERVICE_ID.
